summary:
Biomineralization involves the incorporation of inorganic elements like calcium and phosphate into biological structures, notably forming hydroxyapatite in bones and teeth or calcium carbonate in mollusk shells. Mollusc shells exemplify this process due to active, physiologically regulated formation of calcium-containing minerals within living tissues.  

\boxed{D}
